acceptance_summary: ED2K client identity and runtime metadata needed for queues, credits, and peer recognition are persistent, documented, and compatible with aria2-next session ownership.
Sources have explicit lifecycle state, source caps, quality, retry, dead-source expiry, no-needed-parts handling, and safe dedupe rules.
Server TCP and UDP source requests follow bounded aMule-style cadence and use server capability metadata without noisy steady-state polling.
Kad bootstrap, source search, source publish, refresh, and firewalled checks run as durable maintenance loops when usable state exists.
Peer download state distinguishes connecting, queued, downloading, no-needed-parts, callback waiting, dead, retrying, cancelled, and no-file outcomes.
Queued peers are periodically reasked with OP_REASKFILEPING, and OP_REASKACK, queue-full, and file-not-found responses update peer state and scheduling.
LowID callback requests, accepted callbacks, failures, timeouts, and unreachable boundaries are explicit and isolated from direct peer scheduling.
Block request pacing, transfer timeout, cancel, range cleanup, retry, and stalled-peer handling avoid zero-byte stalls and busy loops.
Sharing, upload queue, UDP reask response, credit persistence, and cooperative peer behavior support the hardened download runtime without false secure-ident claims.

checkpoint: Capability truth docs and final verification
acceptance_summary: Capability claims, RPC/Motrix fields, user docs, maintenance records, and final local verification match the hardened ED2K behavior and remaining limits.
